name: Particle Text
description:
Particle Text renders particle dynamics driven by both a fluid-density force field and a procedural text attractor. The graph is organized into named stages so you can see the entire signal flow at a glance.

The spine is the particle feedback loop. Particle State (array_feedback) holds the previous frame's particles and is updated each frame: Spawn Particles seeds new particles on each clip trigger, Particle State stores them, Move Particles integrates them forward through both forces and sends the result back to Particle State. Render Density splats those particles into a density image.

Two parallel force sources feed the integrator. Density Field takes the rendered density, computes a gradient, scales and rotates it into a curl-flow (divergence-free), and blurs it. Text Force renders the text string, blurs it softly, computes a perpendicular gradient, and scales it by the text_strength slider. Both fields are summed via field_plus_text and sampled per-particle.

Resolution Scaling derives all the resolution-independent numbers the particle pipeline needs: particle count, scatter energy, blur radii, and display intensity, all keyed to canvas size. Clip Triggers controls the behavioral triggers: the gate and envelope, the mode selector, and the four behaviors that can fire on a clip trigger event (turbulence burst, rotation flip, flow reversal, pattern reset, inject burst).

Display zooms and tone-maps the final density to the screen. Everything flattens to byte-identical behavior at load, and card bindings reach every inner node by its stable nodeId regardless of grouping depth.

nodes.title: Seed Pattern
